Pandowdy Apple IIe Emulator
Table of Contents

Class LanguageCard

Namespace
Pandowdy.EmuCore
Assembly
Pandowdy.EmuCore.dll

Manages the Apple IIe Language Card memory banking for the $D000-$FFFF address space.

public class LanguageCard : ILanguageCard, IMemory
Inheritance
object
LanguageCard
Implements
ILanguageCard
IMemory
Inherited Members
object.Equals(object)
object.Equals(object, object)
object.GetHashCode()
object.GetType()
object.MemberwiseClone()
object.ReferenceEquals(object, object)
object.ToString()

Remarks

Purpose: Implements the Language Card banking mechanism that allows 16KB of RAM to be mapped into the normally ROM-occupied address space ($D000-$FFFF), with two switchable 4KB banks for $D000-$DFFF and a shared 8KB region for $E000-$FFFF.

Memory Layout: 

Apple IIe Address Space:
$D000-$DFFF (4KB) - Bank 1 or Bank 2 (switchable)
$E000-$FFFF (8KB) - Common area (shared between banks)

Physical RAM Layout (16KB total):
Bank 1: $0000-$0FFF (4KB) + $2000-$3FFF (8KB) = 12KB
Bank 2: $1000-$3FFF (12KB)

Banking Control: The Language Card reads state from ISystemStatusProvider to determine:

  • StateHighRead - Read from RAM vs ROM
  • StateHighWrite - RAM write enable
  • StateUseBank1 - Bank 1 vs Bank 2 selection
  • StateRamRd - Main vs auxiliary memory for reads
  • StateRamWrt - Main vs auxiliary memory for writes

Design Philosophy: This class is a pure memory accessor that queries current state flags but does not manage state transitions. State management is handled by the soft switch controller, which updates the ISystemStatusProvider. This separation provides clean architecture, optimal performance, and excellent testability.

Performance: All read/write methods use aggressive inlining for maximum performance in this hot path (called hundreds of thousands of times per second).

Thread Safety: Not thread-safe. Assumes single-threaded CPU execution. The ISystemStatusProvider should handle any necessary synchronization.

Constructors

LanguageCard(ISystemRam, ISystemRam?, ISystemRomProvider, IFloatingBusProvider, ISystemStatusProvider)

Manages the Apple IIe Language Card memory banking for the $D000-$FFFF address space.

public LanguageCard(ISystemRam mainRam, ISystemRam? auxRam, ISystemRomProvider systemRom, IFloatingBusProvider floatingBus, ISystemStatusProvider status)

Parameters

mainRam ISystemRam
auxRam ISystemRam
systemRom ISystemRomProvider
floatingBus IFloatingBusProvider
status ISystemStatusProvider

Remarks

Purpose: Implements the Language Card banking mechanism that allows 16KB of RAM to be mapped into the normally ROM-occupied address space ($D000-$FFFF), with two switchable 4KB banks for $D000-$DFFF and a shared 8KB region for $E000-$FFFF.

Memory Layout: 

Apple IIe Address Space:
$D000-$DFFF (4KB) - Bank 1 or Bank 2 (switchable)
$E000-$FFFF (8KB) - Common area (shared between banks)

Physical RAM Layout (16KB total):
Bank 1: $0000-$0FFF (4KB) + $2000-$3FFF (8KB) = 12KB
Bank 2: $1000-$3FFF (12KB)

Banking Control: The Language Card reads state from ISystemStatusProvider to determine:

  • StateHighRead - Read from RAM vs ROM
  • StateHighWrite - RAM write enable
  • StateUseBank1 - Bank 1 vs Bank 2 selection
  • StateRamRd - Main vs auxiliary memory for reads
  • StateRamWrt - Main vs auxiliary memory for writes

Design Philosophy: This class is a pure memory accessor that queries current state flags but does not manage state transitions. State management is handled by the soft switch controller, which updates the ISystemStatusProvider. This separation provides clean architecture, optimal performance, and excellent testability.

Performance: All read/write methods use aggressive inlining for maximum performance in this hot path (called hundreds of thousands of times per second).

Thread Safety: Not thread-safe. Assumes single-threaded CPU execution. The ISystemStatusProvider should handle any necessary synchronization.

Properties

this[ushort]

Gets or sets a byte at the specified address using array-like indexer syntax.

public byte this[ushort address] { get; set; }

Parameters

address ushort

Address to access, typically in the $D000-$FFFF range.

Property Value

byte

Byte value from RAM, ROM, or floating bus.

Remarks

Convenience Syntax: Provides array-like access to the Language Card: 

byte value = languageCard[0xD000];  // Read
languageCard[0xE000] = 0x42;        // Write

Implementation: Getter delegates to Read(ushort), setter delegates to Write(ushort, byte). See those methods for detailed behavior.

Size

Gets the size of the Language Card address space (12KB).

public int Size { get; }

Property Value

int

Always returns 0x3000 (12,288 bytes), representing the $D000-$FFFF address range.

Remarks

Note: The Language Card uses 16KB of physical RAM, but only 12KB is addressable at any given time due to bank switching. The returned size matches the accessible address space ($D000-$FFFF).

Methods

Read(ushort)

Reads a byte from the Language Card address space.

public byte Read(ushort address)

Parameters

address ushort

Address to read from, typically in the $D000-$FFFF range (though any 16-bit address is accepted for calculation purposes).

Returns

byte

Byte value from RAM, ROM, or floating bus, depending on system state.

Remarks

Read Decision Logic: 

If StateHighRead is true:
  If StateRamRd is true:
    Read from auxiliary RAM (or floating bus if aux doesn't exist)
  Else:
    Read from main RAM
Else:
  Read from system ROM

Floating Bus: If auxiliary memory is accessed but doesn't exist, returns the floating bus value instead. This models Apple IIe hardware behavior where unmapped memory reads return the last value on the data bus.

Bank Selection: The StateUseBank1 flag determines which 4KB bank is accessed for $D000-$DFFF. The $E000-$FFFF region is shared.

Performance: Aggressively inlined. Read from main RAM with ROM fallback is the common path and executes in ~7-10 cycles after inlining.

Write(ushort, byte)

Writes a byte to the Language Card address space.

public void Write(ushort address, byte data)

Parameters

address ushort

Address to write to, typically in the $D000-$FFFF range (though any 16-bit address is accepted for calculation purposes).

data byte

Byte value to write.

Remarks

Write Protection: Writes are only executed if StateHighWrite is true. The Language Card uses a two-access sequence to enable writing, preventing accidental ROM overwrites.

Write Decision Logic: 

If StateHighWrite is false:
  Return (no-op, ROM is write-protected)

If StateRamWrt is true:
  Write to auxiliary RAM (or ignore if aux doesn't exist)
Else:
  Write to main RAM

Bank Selection: The StateUseBank1 flag determines which 4KB bank is written to for $D000-$DFFF. The $E000-$FFFF region is shared.

ROM Write Attempts: Writes when StateHighWrite is false are silently ignored (no-op), matching Apple IIe hardware behavior where ROM writes have no effect.

Performance: Aggressively inlined. Write protection check is the fast path (early return), keeping overhead minimal.
